obligations.reason: The scope exposes external JTAG pins, SimDTM/SimJTAG/dmi_jtag instances, debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, and jtag_unlock/jtag_hash signals; visible RTL should be checked to ensure debug access is not merely connected without authorization mediation.
obligations.subject: JTAG/DTM debug requester
obligations.operation: trigger debug request/reset or access debug module
obligations.object: debug capability, hart control, non-debug reset/debug module state
obligations.expected_guard: debug authorization or JTAG unlock/authentication must mediate debug requests before they can request hart debug entry, non-debug reset, or access protected state
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG/SimJTAG/DTM inputs -> dmi_jtag/Debug Module request signals -> debug_req_o/ndmreset_o/dmactive_o and any debug slave/master bus path
state_conditions_to_check: reset state of debug authorization
whether testmode_i or simulation DTM bypasses authentication
whether jtag_unlock gates all debug-relevant outputs
required_evidence_slots: object: debug_req_o/ndmreset_o/dmactive_o or debug module bus registers
observed RTL condition on request path
source locations for request generation and guard use
knowledge_used: evidence closure: subject, operation, object, guard, path
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer shows debug instances and signals but not full port connections or conditional generate context.

obligations.reason: reglk_wrapper has AXI-facing en/we/address/wdata/rdata, jtag_unlock input, and outputs lock controls consumed by other wrappers; lock semantics must be verified at set/clear and use sites.
obligations.subject: software-visible AXI requester and JTAG unlock source
obligations.operation: write, clear, or override lock control
obligations.object: reglk_ctrl / reglk_ctrl_o lock bits controlling peripheral register writes
obligations.expected_guard: writes that set or clear register-lock control must be restricted by privilege/access control and once locked must not be cleared except by authorized reset/lifecycle action or authenticated JTAG unlock if intended
obligations.candidate_path: reglk_wrapper AXI/MMIO write path -> reglk_ctrl_o -> reglk_ctrl consumers in peripheral wrappers
state_conditions_to_check: reset values of reglk_ctrl
whether bits are sticky after set
whether jtag_unlock can clear or bypass without authentication
whether all downstream writes use the appropriate reglk_ctrl bit
required_evidence_slots: subject: AXI/MMIO requester and JTAG unlock source
object: reglk_ctrl bits and downstream protected registers
observed update logic and downstream use sites
obligations.uncertainty: Need visible always_ff/update logic and address decode to determine exact lock behavior.

obligations.reason: acc_ctrl controls peripheral access, including a visible boot ROM gate via acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][0]; the configurability of acc_ctrl is itself security-sensitive and must be protected.
obligations.subject: software-visible AXI requester
obligations.expected_guard: only authorized privileged software should configure access-control policy, and policy writes should be protected by reglk_ctrl/lock state
obligations.candidate_path: acct_wrapper AXI/MMIO write path -> acc_ctrl_o/acc_ctrl -> acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][peripheral] gates in riscv_peripherals
state_conditions_to_check: reset/default acc_ctrl permissions
whether acct_ctrl_i/reglk_ctrl_i gates writes
whether lower privilege can widen its own permissions
whether alias rule acc_ctrl[j*4+i] | (j==5 && acc_ctrl[4*4+i]) creates unintended permission inheritance
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: privilege check and reglk_ctrl lock
source locations for policy use sites
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer shows policy signals and one gate, but not all decodes or write conditions.

obligations.reason: riscv_peripherals routes many peripherals and computes acc_ctrl_c from acc_ctrl; visible RTL should verify that all sensitive request paths are consistently gated, not only rom_req.
obligations.subject: CPU or NoC peripheral requester
obligations.object: protected peripheral request paths and NoC/AXI bridges
obligations.expected_guard: every protected peripheral request should be mediated by the correct acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][peripheral] bit, with no unguarded alternate request path
obligations.candidate_path: priv_lvl_i-indexed acc_ctrl_c gates -> peripheral request enables for bootrom, crypto, debug, CLINT/PLIC, DMA, pkt/acct/reglk
state_conditions_to_check: priv_lvl_i values and array bounds
request gating under reset
consistency between read and write gating
whether debug/test paths bypass acc_ctrl_c
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: acc_ctrl_c indexed by current privilege and peripheral ID
observed request enable logic for each peripheral
any bypass path or direct ready/valid path
obligations.uncertainty: Only one explicit rom_req assignment is visible in facts; other peripheral gates require source inspection.

obligations.reason: pkt_wrapper exposes fuse_req_o, fuse_addr_o, and fuse_rdata_i and has AXI rdata path; fuses are protected assets and require mediation and exposure-path review.
obligations.subject: software-visible AXI requester through pkt_wrapper
obligations.expected_guard: fuse access and fuse data exposure should require privilege/access-control and lock mediation; fuse address selection should be constrained to intended ranges
obligations.candidate_path: pkt_wrapper AXI/MMIO path -> fuse_req_o/fuse_addr_o -> fuse_rdata_i -> rdata/NoC response
state_conditions_to_check: whether fuse_req is generated on reads only or writes too
whether fuse_addr can be arbitrary
whether rdata returns fuse_rdata without masking
reset/stale rdata residue after fuse access
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: acct_ctrl_i/reglk_ctrl_i/privilege or range check
obligations.uncertainty: Exact address map and data mux behavior must be inspected.

obligations.reason: dma_wrapper contains start, length, source/destination address registers, core_lock_reg, valid/done/end, and AXI-facing write logic; DMA can bypass CPU privilege if not range-checked and locked.
obligations.subject: software-visible AXI requester and DMA engine as bus master
obligations.object: DMA source/destination/length/start configuration and memory access capability
obligations.expected_guard: DMA configuration and start must be restricted by access-control/lock state, and DMA source/destination/length must be checked against permitted address ranges or policy before transfer
obligations.candidate_path: dma_wrapper AXI/MMIO configuration registers -> DMA start/valid/control signals -> memory/fabric master path
state_conditions_to_check: reset defaults of start/valid/core_lock
whether config can change while transfer active
whether core_lock_reg actually blocks all relevant writes
source/destination range overlap and bounds checks
required_evidence_slots: operation: write config/start and perform transfer
object: DMA registers and target memory regions
expected guard: reglk_ctrl/acct_ctrl and address-range/PMP-like checks
observed write enables/start condition/fabric request logic
race or update behavior during active transfer
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer lists config registers but not transfer engine connections or range checks.

obligations.reason: riscv_peripherals declares multiple rst_* controls and wrappers take local reset inputs; reset paths can clear locks/policies or expose stale outputs if unmediated.
obligations.subject: software-visible reset requester
obligations.object: peripheral reset controls and security state affected by reset
obligations.expected_guard: software-triggered peripheral resets should be writable only by authorized privilege/access policy and should not clear security locks or policy in a way that bypasses protection
obligations.candidate_path: reset-control NoC/MMIO path -> rst_1..rst_13 signals -> peripheral wrapper rst_* inputs
state_conditions_to_check: whether rst_* writes are gated by acc_ctrl/reglk
whether resets clear lock bits or access-control policy
whether stale rdata/output valid remains after reset
whether reset defaults are permissive
required_evidence_slots: object: peripheral state, reglk_ctrl, acc_ctrl, fuse/DMA state
observed reset register decode and effects
obligations.uncertainty: Source lines around reset decode/use must be inspected.

obligations.reason: The wrappers expose rdata on AXI-facing interfaces for security-sensitive state; both write and read permissions need checking to avoid information exposure or policy disclosure where protected.
obligations.subject: software-visible AXI requester
obligations.object: rdata readback of access-control, lock, DMA address/control, pkey/fuse-related registers
obligations.expected_guard: readback of protected policy, lock, DMA address, fuse/key-related, or internal state should be limited to authorized requesters or return masked values when locked
obligations.candidate_path: wrapper AXI read muxes rdata in acct_wrapper/reglk_wrapper/dma_wrapper/pkt_wrapper -> ariane_axi output/NoC response
state_conditions_to_check: read behavior when locked
read behavior after reset or operation completion
whether stale secret/fuse data remains in rdata
whether read and write address decodes are consistent
required_evidence_slots: observed read mux and address decode
obligations.uncertainty: Need exact read mux cases and whether objects are intended readable.

obligations.reason: Top-level we_flag inputs and wrapper we_flag ports may represent external or test write enables; visible RTL should ensure they do not bypass normal permission mediation.
obligations.subject: external/test write-enable flag source
obligations.object: protected wrapper registers controlled by we_flag inputs
obligations.expected_guard: external/test write-enable flags must not independently enable writes to protected registers unless combined with normal address decode, privilege/access-control, and lock checks
obligations.candidate_path: we_flag_0..we_flag_4/top-level we_flag inputs -> wrapper write-enable logic -> protected register updates
state_conditions_to_check: whether we_flag is active during reset/testmode
whether we_flag can write when AXI we is false
whether lock bits are ignored when we_flag is asserted
required_evidence_slots: object: acct/dma or other protected registers
expected guard: address decode plus access-control/lock checks
observed combination of we_flag with we/en/reglk_ctrl
source locations for each we_flag fanout
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer lists ports but not semantic origin or fanout for all flags.
